title: alchemilla alpina var. alpigena
intro: Alchemilla alpina var. alpigena, also known as Alpine Lady's Mantle, is a perennial flowering plant in the rose family native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce swelling, and protect against cell damage. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla alpina var. alpigena have been used to treat various health issues, including eczema, acne, and wounds, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used topically to apply its medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
